party.description: <p>Established under the Western Australian Government's Centre of Excellence and Innovation Program in 1999, the Centre for Isotope Research is named in honour of one of Western Australia's pioneering leaders in the field of isotope research and science education, Curtin Professor John de Laeter (1933-2010). The John De Laeter Centre for Isotope Research supports the sustainable development of Western Australia through the provision of skilled graduates and high-level scientific support that contribute to the growing needs of an innovative industrial and government sector.</p>

name.namePart: Faculty of Science and Engineering, Curtin University

party.description: Faculty of Science and Engineering, Curtin University

name.namePart: John de Laeter Centre, Curtin University

party.description: <p>Established under the Western Australian Government's Centre of Excellence and Innovation Program in 1999, the Centre is named in honour of one of Western Australia's pioneering leaders in the field of isotope research and science education, Curtin Professor John de Laeter (1933-2010). The John De Laeter Centre for Isotope Research supports the sustainable development of Western Australia through the provision of skilled graduates and high-level scientific support that contribute to the growing needs of an innovative industrial and government sector.</p>

party.description: School of Public Health, Curtin University
PhD candidate at Curtin University

<p>David has a PhD in Software Engineering looking at the human aspect of software development, how software developers think. David has spent extensive time in the International community working with NGOs and the education sector.</p>

<p>Research interests in the field of Assistive Technology, particularly Indoor Navigation for people with vision impairment and methods to allow access to education for people with sensory disabilities (print disabilities, hearing and vision loss)</p>

name.namePart: Department of Chemistry, New York University

party.description: Department of Chemistry, New York University

<p>The Nanochemistry Research Institute (NRI) at Curtin University is currently led by Director, Professor Andrew B. Lowe. It is comprised of academic staff members, postdoctoral fellows, as well as PhD, Honours and 3rd project students. The research undertaken by the group ranges from government-funded fundamental research to confidential one-on-one industrial projects.</p>

Professor Andrew Rohl is a world recognized leader in the field of computer simulation of surfaces. He has focused on the simulation of surface interactions in growing crystals but the methods and programs that he has developed are directly relevant to all areas of materials science and nanotechnology. His research philosophy is based around world wide collaborative research both with other theoreticians and more importantly with experimental groups. His success in this endeavour is demonstrated by his large number of publications with world-leading research groups, his major roles in 2 Cooperative Research Centres and bringing together researchers from Curtin University and the University of Oxford, leading to several patents. His leadership style is very consultative, allowing him to successfully lead projects outside his molecular modeling specialty, particularly with industry. In 2004, he became the CEO of iVEC, 'The hub of advanced computing in Western Australia'. His skills have been utilized at iVEC to develop a successful partnership across five institutions that provides major advanced computing facilities for all Western Australian researchers.

name.namePart: Science and Mathematics Education Centre (SMEC), Curtin University

party.description: SMEC operates within the School of Education in the Humanities Faculty. The Centre offers postgraduate studies in science, mathematics and technology education and has a national and international reputation for excellence in research and development.

The research carried out in the Department of Chemistry encompasses both fundamental and applied research, with a strong focus on interdisciplinary science and collaboration with industry

PhD candidate at University of Bath

name.namePart: Department of Chemistry, University of Bath

party.description: Department of Chemistry, University of Bath

<p>Research in our group concerns the development and application of theoretical or computational methods to problems in chemistry, physics, geochemistry, mineralogy and materials science. Current foci include; polymorphism, crystallisation, mineral-water interfaces, fuel cells, lithium battery materials, solvent extraction, desalination membranes.</p>

name.namePart: Centre for Marine Science and Technology, Curtin University

party.description: Founded in 1985, the Centre for Marine Science and Technology (CMST) comprises a multi-skilled group of scientists and engineers committed to the development of technical ocean-related skills in Australia. The Centre has earned a reputation as a high quality marine technology research and development facility responsive to industry and government needs. CMSTĂ•s expertise fits into four major categories: Hydrodynamics, Underwater Acoustics, Marine Ecology and Stereoscopic Imaging.
PhD candidate at Curtin University

name.namePart: School of Psychology and Speech Pathology, Curtin University

party.description: School of Psychology and Speech Pathology, Curtin University

<p>The Geological Survey of Western Australia (GSWA) collects, manages and shares information about geology and geophysics of Western Australia to promote investment in the resources sector leading to exploration and extraction of minerals, bulk commodities, oil and gas. Activities include the production of maps, reports and scientific publications that contain interpretations of tectonic setting, crustal architecture and geological evolution.</p>

<p>The Geological Survey of Western Australia (GSWA) collects, manages and shares information about geology and geophysics of Western Australia to promote investment in the resources sector leading to exploration and extraction of minerals, bulk commodities, oil and gas. Activities include the production of maps, reports and scientific publications that contain interpretations of tectonic setting, crustal architecture and geological evolution.</p>

party.description: The school is a combination of studies in agriculture, chemistry, medical radiation sciences, environmental sciences and food science. Research is closely aligned with teaching and industry.

Sir Charles Gairdner Hospital (SCGH) is one of Australia 's leading teaching tertiary hospitals.

name.namePart: School of Pharmacy and Biomedical Sciences, Curtin University

party.description: The school offers a range of undergraduate courses, including the only accredited pharmacy course in Western Australia, an AIMS-accredited program in laboratory medicine, and courses in molecular genetics and biotechnology, human biology preclinical, and oral health therapy. We provide postgraduate courses in pharmacy and biomedical sciences.

name.namePart: School of Media,Creative Arts and Social Inquiry, Curtin University

party.description: School of Media,Creative Arts and Social Inquiry is renowned for its unique, ground-breaking combinations of critical and creative research in the domains of digital futures, social justice, international relations, and cultural industries.

ChemCentre is a Western Australian statutory authority which provides specialised chemical and forensic services to ensure a safe and prosperous Western Australian community.

name.namePart: School of Earth and Planetary Sciences, Curtin University

party.description: School of Earth and Planetary Sciences provides world-class training for geoscientific and geospatial careers through our industry-recognised teaching programs, and undertake fundamental and applied research into the past, present and future of our planet and solar system.
<p>Masters of Research Student</p>
<p>School of Earth and Planetary Sciences, Curtin University; PhD Candidate</p>

<p>The Surveying & Spatial Sciences Institute (SSSI) is Australia's peak body representing the interests of surveying and spatial science professionals, combining the disciplines of land surveying, engineering & mining surveying, cartography, hydrography, remote sensing and spatial information science.</p>
